text.data-narration: E-Waste refers to any electronic device, component, or part that wastes electrical energy when discarded, including:

Computers, printers, scanners, and other office equipment, televisions, air conditioners, refrigerators, and other household appliances, mobile phones, tablets, and other portable electronic devices, batteries and other electrical components, any waste generated from the production, packaging, transportation, and disposal of electronic products.
Increased consumption of electronic devices, obsolescence of electronic devices, rapid technological advancements, lack of effective e-waste management infrastructure
Pollution of soil, water, and air, harm to human health and the environment, loss of natural resources and energy, economic losses due to the disposal of hazardous materials
Identification and segregation, collection, transportation, processing, recycling, disposal

Identify e-waste and segregate it from other types of waste, collect e-waste from households, offices, and other sources, transport e-waste to designated e-waste collection centers, process e-waste into raw materials, recycle materials from e-waste into new products, dispose of non-recyclable materials from e-waste in a responsible manner

Prohibition on export of e-waste, regulation of e-waste collection, establishment of e-waste management infrastructure, recycling and reuse of e-waste materials, disposal of hazardous e-waste materials

Prohibition on export of e-waste to countries with inadequate e-waste management infrastructure, regulation of e-waste collection from households, offices, and other sources, establishment of e-waste management infrastructure, recycling and reuse of e-waste materials, disposal of hazardous e-waste materials in a responsible manner
